rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical four-component domino [1+2+1+1] cycloaddition of aldehydes, styrenes, tert-butyl nitrite, and H2O to give 3,5-disubstituted isoxazoles
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of bromide (from n-Bu4NBr electrolyte) to Br radical, which serves as a HAT mediator to abstract the aldehyde C-H and generate an acyl radical; cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: bromine radical (Br•) from anodic oxidation of Br-
acyl radical A from aldehyde HAT
tert-butoxy radical II from TBN homolysis
N=O radical III from TBN homolysis
N=O radical IV generated in situ from TBN + H2O via HNO2
carbon-centered radical B from acyl radical addition to styrene

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Br- to Br•, which abstracts the aldehyde C-H to form an acyl radical that adds to styrene; the resulting C-radical combines with an N=O radical (from TBN homolysis and/or TBN+H2O→HNO2→N=O) to give a β-carbonyl nitroso/oxime intermediate that cyclodehydrates (TFA-assisted) to the isoxazole, while the cathode evolves H2.
rate_determining_step: Step involving alkene 2 and TBN 3 (first-order in [alkene] and [TBN], zero-order in aldehyde), consistent with radical addition/N=O trapping being rate-limiting

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Yield drops to 3%, indicating radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction suppressed (trace); BHT-N=O adduct 6 isolated and confirmed by HR-MS, supporting N=O radical

probe_reagents.name: D2O (replacing H2O)
probe_reagents.observation: Product m/z shifts 246→247; 88% D incorporation at C4 of isoxazole, showing H at C4 comes from water

probe_reagents.name: DMF-d7
probe_reagents.observation: No D incorporation (m/z 246), excluding DMF as H source

probe_reagents.name: H2-18O
probe_reagents.observation: m/z 246→248 (100%), showing isoxazole O partly originates from H2O (in addition to TBN)

probe_reagents.name: 1,3-diketone 10 (presumed intermediate)
probe_reagents.observation: Subjected to standard conditions: no isoxazole formed, excluding 1,3-dicarbonyl pathway

probe_reagents.name: β-carbonyl ketoxime 11
probe_reagents.observation: Gave isoxazole 7 in 91% yield, supporting ketoxime D as on-cycle intermediate

notes: Two parallel sources of N=O are proposed: direct TBN homolysis (pathway III) and TBN + H2O → HNO2 → N=O (pathway IV); 18O-labeling shows both TBN and water contribute the ring O. CV shows that mixing 1a+2a+3 lowers the oxidation potential of 1a from 0.99 V to 0.73 V vs SCE, supporting anodic involvement. α-Methylstyrene fails (steric effect); no reaction without current.
